explanations.4: This loads a helper library called bats-support that provides utility functions for tests.
explanations.5: This loads another helper library called bats-assert that gives us the assert_* commands we use below.
explanations.9: The 'run' command executes the following command and captures its output and exit code for us to check.
explanations.16: The --regexp flag means we're checking the output against a pattern, not an exact string match; the pattern allows either uppercase or lowercase S.
explanations.22: We're intentionally running a command that will fail (removing a non-existent file) to test that our code properly detects failures.
explanations.23: This asserts that the previous command failed (returned a non-zero exit code), which is what we expect when deleting a file that doesn't exist.
